name: Coord primary-trees (admin)
description: Admin primary-tree state view (/admin/coord/trees) from plan 2026-05-19-coordinator-production-readiness Phase 2, extended by 2026-05-30-coord-pull-decision-ui (the TreeCard pull-decision badges). ci-bot is a superuser so the page renders its real (non-redirect) content. The page seeds its device_id from `?device_id=` and fetches GET /api/v1/operations/trees/by-device/<deviceId>, rendering one TreeCard per row; the contention view is behind the 'Contention' tab and is NOT mounted on initial load (so this spec does not assert it). metadata.routeStubs fulfills the by-device endpoint with three canned rows engineered to exercise the full TreeCard badge vocabulary via the real `pullSafetyClass` ladder (TreeCard.tsx:59): row 1 (main, behind 3, clean) → `pull` verdict; row 2 (main, behind 2, local_ahead 2) → `diverged` verdict + `ahead` badge; row 3 (feature branch, behind 0, dirty) → `up_to_date` verdict (behind<=0 short-circuits) + `dirty` badge. The stub is load-bearing: remove it and the sentinel device returns zero rows → all coord-tree-* card/badge assertions red, leaving only the chrome states. Authored from a live authed in-process UI Bridge snapshot (1099 instrumented elements, route /admin/coord/trees) captured 2026-06-04. Read-only; structural only.

states.name: Coord console shell
states.description: The persistent coord operator-console chrome that renders on every /admin/coord/* route once the superuser gate passes: the h1 title and the left coord-nav with its Trees and Pull Decisions links. Identifies the page mounted under the coord layout, independent of any tree data.

assertions.description: h1 'Coord operator console' — the console title proving the coord layout mounted (superuser gate passed, not redirected).
'Trees' nav link (data-testid coord-nav-trees) — the active section.
'Pull Decisions' nav link (data-testid coord-nav-pull-decisions) — cross-link to the sibling decision feed the TreeCard verdict badges deep-link into.

states.name: By-device panel controls
states.description: The 'Primary trees by device' panel chrome that renders whenever the by-device tab is active (the default), independent of whether the fetch returned rows: the two view tabs (By device / Contention), the device_id input, and the refresh button. These render even on an empty/error fetch, so they are the stable structural signal that the by-device panel mounted.

assertions.description: 'Contention' view tab (data-testid coord-trees-tab-contention) — the alternate view (its panel is not mounted until activated, so this spec asserts the tab, not the panel).

states.name: Populated tree cards + pull-decision badges (route-stubbed)
states.description: The TreeCard rows that render only when the by-device fetch returns trees, and the full pull-decision badge vocabulary they surface (the 2026-05-30-coord-pull-decision-ui deliverable). With metadata.routeStubs fulfilling the by-device endpoint with three engineered rows, the real `pullSafetyClass` ladder produces: a `pull` verdict badge (row 1: main/behind-3/clean), a `diverged` verdict plus an `ahead` badge (row 2: main/behind-2/local_ahead-2), and an `up_to_date` verdict plus a `dirty` badge (row 3: feature-branch/behind-0/dirty). This state is load-bearing on the stub: remove it and the sentinel device returns zero rows, dropping every coord-tree-* assertion to the empty-state placeholder.

assertions.description: At least one TreeCard (data-testid coord-tree-card) rendered from the stubbed by-device rows.
'dirty' working-tree badge (data-testid coord-tree-dirty-badge) — row 3 has dirty=true.
'N ahead' unpushed-commits badge (data-testid coord-tree-ahead-badge) — row 2 has local_ahead=2.
'pull' pull-safety verdict badge (data-testid coord-tree-verdict-pull) — row 1 is ff-only-safe (main, behind 3, clean).
'up to date' pull-safety verdict badge (data-testid coord-tree-verdict-up_to_date) — row 3 has behind_count 0.
